description_long: This rifle features a compact, tactical design with a dark green and black color scheme. The stock is skeletonized, providing a lightweight yet sturdy rear support, and the grip is ergonomically shaped for comfortable handling. The barrel is relatively short and robust, with a cylindrical muzzle device at the end, likely for flash suppression or recoil reduction. The upper receiver has a boxy, angular cover, and a detachable box magazine is inserted below the main body. There is a simple, blocky sighting system mounted on top, suitable for quick target acquisition in urban environments. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal with some polymer components for weight reduction and durability.
description: A compact, tactical rifle with a skeletonized stock, short barrel, detachable box magazine, and a blocky sight, finished in dark green and black.
description_view_0: Side view showing the skeletonized stock, ergonomic grip, detachable box magazine, and the full length of the short barrel with its muzzle device.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin-barrel appearance, muzzle device, and the alignment of the sighting system.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same key features as view 0, with clearer visibility of the magazine and stock structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the open structure of the skeletonized stock and the back of the receiver.
